flash_cards.topic_name: Geometrical Meaning of the Zeroes of a Polynomial (X-2.1)

cards.text: The zeroes of a polynomial p(x) are precisely the x-coordinates of the points, where the graph of y = p(x) intersects the x -axis.
cards.topic_name: Geometrical Meaning of the Zeroes of a Polynomial (X-2.1)

cards.text: A quadratic polynomial in x with real coefficients is of the form ax2 + bx + c, where a, b, c are real numbers with a {tex}\neq{/tex} 0.
cards.topic_name: Geometrical Meaning of the Zeroes of a Polynomial (X-2.1)

cards.text:
If {tex}\alpha{/tex}, {tex}\beta{/tex}, {tex}\gamma{/tex} are the zeroes of the cubic polynomial ax3 + bx2 + cx + d, then
{tex}\alpha+\beta+\gamma=\frac{-b}{a},{/tex} {tex}\alpha \beta+\beta \gamma+\gamma \alpha=\frac{c}{a},{/tex} and {tex}\alpha \beta \gamma=\frac{-d}{a}{/tex}.

If {tex}\alpha{/tex} and {tex}\beta{/tex} are the zeroes of the quadratic polynomial ax2 + bx + c, then
{tex}\alpha{/tex} + {tex}\beta{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{b}{a}{/tex}, {tex}\alpha{/tex}{tex}\beta{/tex} = {tex}\frac{c}{a}{/tex}.
